title: Free gifts: capitalism and the politics of nature [Book Review]
author: Slothuus

opening_move.description: The review opens with Battistoni's core argument: capitalism treats nature as a 'free gift' — unpaid for in the economic accounting — structurally, not accidentally, and this failure underpins ecological crisis.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: Understanding capitalism's structural devaluation of nature (alongside other 'free gifts' — reproductive labor, atmosphere) is essential for ecological politics

rivals_engaged: critical approaches that treat nature-devaluation as oversight rather than structure
theoretical_positioning.description: The review presents Battistoni's argument appreciatively, contextualising it within Marxist political economy and feminist theory.

epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: Marxist political economy; feminist theory; political ecology
epistemic_mode.notes: Book review genre; primarily expository. Battistoni's book itself uses historical and theoretical analysis of capitalist production.

case_type.case_name: Battistoni, Free Gifts (2025); capitalist value chains

confidence_notes: Book review genre; primarily expository. The normative stake derives from the reviewed book's political ecology argument.
